lever: tournament.py re-exported nx's is_reachable (import *), so it ran nx's O(V^3) closed-set scan directly on the fnx graph, hammering fnx's slow ._adj/._pred accessors thousands of times (3.9x slower than nx). Override with an fnx-native version that snapshots successor/predecessor adjacency into plain set dicts ONCE, then runs nx's EXACT algorithm on them. Deterministic boolean -> byte-exact, no per-access accessor tax.

parity.coverage: random tournaments (all-True, strongly connected) + transitive/near-transitive tournaments (False cases) + undirected/multigraph NetworkXNotImplemented parity

headline: tournament.is_reachable: 3.9x slower than nx -> ~1.2x near-parity, 2.65-3.69x self-speedup (grows with n), byte-exact. Snapshot adjacency once instead of letting nx's O(V^3) scan hammer fnx accessors. Pure Python, no rebuild.
